behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real vision-model judgment of explicitness; a stubbed classifier would hide misclassification risk. Rejected Workflow because behavior text specifies a single model decision plus policy action, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Submit known explicit images to the real vision model path and verify the moderation outcome is `held_for_review` and never transitions to published.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: This is model robustness/quality (semantic detection under obfuscation), which unit/integration with canned outputs cannot validate. Rejected Workflow because only one agentic classifier decision is required by the behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run an obfuscation corpus through the real text moderation model and assert posts are flagged/held across substitution and unicode-lookalike variants.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is cross-module handoff and decision wiring (image result + text result -> publish path). Behavior text disqualifies Agentic because it assumes checks already pass; it does not require validating model output quality. Rejected System because no explicit requirement for real external HTTP/OS/subprocess or external contract verification.
behaviors.test_description: Wire moderation orchestrator, classifiers (passing fixtures), and persistence/publisher modules together; verify post state becomes published and is present in the publish store/outbox, not held or dropped.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires multiple agentic steps in sequence (image model then text model then decision), and end-to-end latency across that chain. Rejected lower levels because unit/integration fakes miss real model-call timing and sequencing effects.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end submissions through real image+text model calls and decisioning, measuring submit-to-final-decision latency; assert p95 (or required percentile) stays under 5 seconds.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: This is infrastructure/error-handling orchestration around the model call (failure -> retry queue), not model output quality. Rejected Agentic due behavior text focusing on unavailability path, not classification correctness. Rejected System because behavior does not explicitly require real network/subprocess error contracts; controlled failure injection across modules is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Integrate orchestrator, vision-client adapter, and retry queue; inject model-unavailable errors from the adapter and verify posts are queued with retry metadata and never auto-approved.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: Consistency for borderline content depends on real model behavior across repeated runs; deterministic stubs cannot expose variance-related failures. Rejected Workflow because this is repeated evaluation of one agentic decision point, not a multi-stage agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly within the target window through the real vision moderation path and assert decision consistency meets the defined threshold/policy.
